urn:contentItem:6JGW-R703-S0NX-0063-00000-00ALBERICI CORPORATION - PEER INSIGHTS: INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS & KEY TRENDS2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:07ZLexisNexisSTOCKALBERICI CORPORATION - PEER INSIGHTS: INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS & KEY TRENDS

DASHBOARD: ALBERICI CORPORATION

PermID5000372176Websitehttp://www.alberici.comIndustryConstructionAddress120 S Central Ave CLAYTON MISSOURI 63105-1705 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Alberici Corporation, founded in 1918, is a construction and engineering firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 2,000 employees, it serves automotive, energy, and healthcare sectors, generating approximately $2 billion annually. Alberici provides design-build, construction management, and general contracting, with notable projects like the Gateway Arch visitor center renovation. Its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices enhances efficiency. In 2024, Alberici expanded into renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. The firms culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and employee training, fostering long-term client partnerships. Competing with Turner Construction, Alberici stands out for its quality and environmental stewardship, driving growth in sustainable construction.

+ ALBERICI CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ALBERICI CORPORATION PROGRESSIVE DESIGN-BUILD IN ACTION: DELIVERING CRITICAL WATER INFRASTRUCTURE AT INDUSTRIAL SPEED

As industrial growth and aging infrastructure place new demands on water systems, project teams are rethinking how critical water infrastructure is delivered. Progressive design-build reduces risk, accelerates delivery, and unlocks innovative solutions on complex water projects.

Source: Company Website

+ ALBERICI CORPORATION WRIGHT CITY UNVEILS NEW WASTEWATER TREATMENT FACILITY

WRIGHT CITY, Mo. - A new wastewater treatment facility is now complete in Wright City, Mo., marking a major step forward in supporting regional growth and sustainability. The project was delivered by the joint venture design-build team of Alberici and Woodard & Curran for the Public Water Supply District No. 2 (PWSD No. 2) in partnership with American Foods Group (AFG).

The new facility expands treatment capacity from 500,000 gallons per day to 3.5 million gallons per day, providing advanced nutrient removal for both municipal and industrial flows. It replaces an older system and supports operations at AFG's new America's Heartland Packaging plant while accommodating future residential and commercial growth.

"The Alberici-Woodard & Curran team worked closely with PWSD No. 2 and AFG to bring this project to life," said John Grib, Vice President at Alberici. "Delivering a complex facility like this ahead of schedule and with zero safety incidents is a true reflection of the collaboration and commitment shared by everyone involved."

The project achieved substantial completion one week ahead of schedule and logged more than 321,000 work hours with zero recordable or lost-time injuries.

PWSD No. 2, AFG, and local leaders recently gathered to celebrate the ribbon cutting at the new facility, located at 1000 Lagoon Drive in Wright City.

Source: Company Website

+ ALBERICI CORPORATION SARONIC EXPANDS ITS LOUISIANA SHIPYARD WITH $300 MILLION INVESTMENT TO ACCELERATE AUTONOMOUS SHIP PRODUCTION

Franklin, Louisiana - Saronic today announced a $300 million investment to expand its Franklin, Louisiana shipyard - a major milestone in the company's continued growth as a leader in autonomous shipbuilding. This investment will significantly increase production capacity for Saronic's fleet of Autonomous Surface Vessels (ASVs), adding 1,500 skilled jobs and strengthening the Gulf Coast's role in the future of American maritime innovation in the process.

Supported by close partnerships with Louisiana Economic Development, St. Mary Parish, and other state and local officials, Saronic is expanding its facility to meet surging demand for its autonomous ships. The company broke ground on the new construction project in November 2025, which will add more than 300,000 square feet of new production capacity. The project is slated for completion by the end of 2026, with expanded operations coming online in early 2027.

"This expansion represents what American industry can achieve when innovation and production are fully aligned," said Dino Mavrookas, Saronic Co-founder and CEO. "Our expanded shipyard will enable us to deliver autonomous ships at unprecedented speed and scale while creating 1,500 high-quality jobs across Louisiana. Together with our next-generation Port Alpha shipyard, we're establishing the modern blueprint for American shipbuilding - an integrated ecosystem that connects autonomy innovation with large-scale production capacity to strengthen and sustain America's maritime leadership for generations to come."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ NNSA ANNOUNCES APPROVAL TO BEGIN FULL CONSTRUCTION OF SRS SPECIALIZED TRAINING FACILITY FOR PLUTONIUM PIT PRODUCTION VIEW ARTICLE

The U.S. Department of Energy's National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA) has announced approval of start of construction and established a project baseline, known as Critical Decision (CD)-2/3 approval, for the High-Fidelity Training and Operations Center (HFTOC) at the Savannah River Site (SRS).

The HFTOC will mimic the capabilities in the Savannah River Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF) Main Process Building and allow operators to develop pit production competencies on "like-for-like" sets of equipment with surrogate materials. The capabilities at HFTOC are essential to develop SRS' pit production workforce and will expedite the production of war reserve plutonium pits once SRPPF comes online.

Source: Company Website

+ PROJET TRAMCITE SELECTS CONSTRUCTION KIEWIT CIE FOR UNDERGROUND UTILITY RELOCATION WORK

Projet TramCite has reached a new milestone with the selection of Kiewit, supported by CIMA+, to carry out underground utility relocation work on Rene-Levesque Boulevard and 1st Avenue in Quebec City. This work represents a key preparatory phase for the implementation of the future tramway.

Kiewit was selected following a rigorous and structured procurement process conducted in accordance with the highest industry standards. The company will rely on the technical expertise and execution capacity of its teams to deliver this complex scope of work. Active in Quebec since 1957, Kiewit has an established track record in the delivery of major infrastructure projects, including the reconstruction of the Turcot Interchange in Montreal, the extension of Highway 25, and the construction of hydroelectric facilities.

The underground utility relocation work will prepare two strategic downtown corridors for the future tramway. Particular attention will be paid to managing impacts and implementing mitigation measures for residents, businesses, and users of the area.

Source: Company Website

+ OGLETHORPE POWER SELECTS KIEWIT SUBSIDIARY FOR NEW MONROE COUNTY COMBINED-CYCLE PLANT

Tucker, GA (January 13, 2026) - Today, Oglethorpe Power announced it has reached another major milestone in the development of its new combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Monroe County, officially selecting Kiewit Corporation through its subsidiary, The Industrial Company (TIC), as the Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) partner for the project. Owned by and serving 38 Electric Membership Cooperatives (EMCs) across Georgia, Oglethorpe Power is developing the new facility to support long-term, system-wide load growth across its cooperative membership, including homes, businesses and existing industries-not to serve any single customer or standalone project.

"We are proud to partner with Kiewit through its subsidiary TIC for this critical energy project that will support our mission to deliver safe, reliable and affordable energy to our Members and the millions of Georgians they serve," said Annalisa Bloodworth, Oglethorpe Power President & CEO. "Its proven success in delivering complex power projects safely, on schedule and within budget, combined with a commitment to safety and community, makes the company an ideal partner for this facility and aligns perfectly with our cooperative principles and dedication to Monroe County."

The TIC team is expected to mobilize in Spring 2026, with commercial operation of the new facility anticipated in 2029. Once online, the 1,425-megawatt plant will provide reliable, efficient electricity to homes and businesses across Georgia served by Oglethorpe Power's Member cooperatives.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION HASLAM SPORTS GROUP, AECOM HUNT AND TURNER JV BREAK GROUND ON HUNTINGTON BANK FIELD

Construction of New State-of-the-Art Facility to Serve as Ohio's First Enclosed Stadium Revolutionary Design by HKS to Bring Football Fans Closer to the Field Than EverThe Haslam Sports Group (HSG) alongside a joint venture of AECOM Hunt and Turner Construction Company and HKS today announced the groundbreaking of the new Huntington Bank Field, the future home of the Cleveland Browns starting in the 2029 season. The groundbreaking ceremony held at the new site in Brook Park today marks the official start of construction on Northeast Ohio's largest economic development project to date, the new Huntington Bank Field enclosed stadium and surrounding district.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WHERE SMALL CHANGES CREATE REAL IMPACT

Turner's support to the Paraplegic Foundation reflects a focus on improving everyday spaces at Sanjeevandeep Special School. The initiative has contributed to creating a more accessible, comfortable environment for children and caregivers.Our recent association with the Paraplegic Foundation came from a shared belief that everyone deserves the opportunity to live with dignity, comfort, and as much independence as possible. Through their work, the Foundation supports initiatives like Sanjeevandeep Special School, a space dedicated to children with special needs, where care, learning, and rehabilitation come together in a very real, hands-on way.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: CONNECTING YOUNG TALENT WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS

As part of our Young Professionals Employee Resource Group initiatives, we hosted the first session of our Coffee Talks with Industry Leaders series, with the participation of Arup Turkiye Country Manager Tulay Hatirnaz, Associate Director Duygu Tokalakoglu, and Associate Aydan Basyazicioglu. Held on April 10 at the Arup Istanbul office, the session brought together our young professionals from across Turkiye. Our esteemed speakers shared their industry experience, career journeys, and leadership perspectives, while also engaging with participants through an interactive Q&A.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. [4 Press Releases]

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES TOPS OUT GILEAD SCIENCES' NEW TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT CENTER IN FOSTER CITY

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. (McCarthy), recently celebrated the topping out of the New Technical Development Center (NTDC) at Gilead Sciences' headquarters in Foster City, marking a major construction milestone with the completion of the building's structural frame. The work is in collaboration with design partners DGA Architects, Salas O'Brien Structural Engineers and civil designer BKF Engineer.

Project partners, field teams and leaders gathered on site to celebrate the center's topping out, marking placement of the final beam and the structure reaching full height. The centuries-old tradition honors the collective effort behind the milestone and signals that structural work is nearing completion as the project moves into its next phase.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: EXPANDING HOSPITAL PARKING STRUCTURES FOR BETTER CARE

Driving your child to the hospital is already a harrowing experience. Finding parking once you arrive should not be your biggest concern. When parking structures prioritize flexibility, enhance connectivity, and respond to evolving patient and staff needs, they make hospital visits a little less daunting.

In early 2025, as McCarthy Building Companies completed the Southwest Tower at Children's Hospital of Orange County (CHOC), in Orange, California, the construction firm also expanded the facility's parking structure. This project involved expanding the structure from 522,000 to 912,000 square feet by adding three vertical levels as well as another horizontal bay to connect the new outpatient tower and the existing main campus. However, the expansion was about more than connecting buildings. It increased the efficiency and sustainability of the overall structure by accommodating a growing number of patients and staff within this care-centric environment.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: BRIGHT FUTURE: A'JEYA ANTHONY'S PATH FROM NSBE TO MCCARTHY

Our goal to be the best builder in America requires an inclusive talent acquisition strategy that enables us to find and recruit the best talent. By expanding our reach through organizations like the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), we can connect with emerging leaders and create more pathways into our industry.

A'Jeya Anthony is a three-time project intern at McCarthy Building Companies. Her journey is just one example of how meeting top candidates where they are help us to connect with exceptional people who strengthen our workforce and business.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. RYAN HOUSE DEBUTS TEEN SUITES FOR KIDS LIVING LONGER WITH LIFE-LIMITING CONDITIONS

Ryan House, a nonprofit that provides respite and end-of-life care to children with life-limiting conditions, recently capped off its 15th anniversary year by unveiling two newly renovated teen suites. The renovations allow Ryan House to better serve children who are now surviving well into adolescence and young adulthood and were made possible through generous support from McCarthy Building Companies, which selected Ryan House as a recipient of the company's Heart Hats(TradeMark) grants, and ARC Document Solutions.

Thanks to advances in diagnostics, disease management, and comprehensive care, many children with life-limiting conditions are now living longer than once expected. Ryan House is evolving alongside the children and families it serves, making meaningful changes to better support teens through environments designed specifically for this stage of life.

The renovation was spearheaded by McCarthy's Specialized Solutions Group, which focuses on complex renovations and technical builds within healthcare and other specialized environments. The donation involved the renovation of two private suites specifically designed for teenagers and features superhero-themed decor provided by ARC that fully immerses them in a comic book-inspired atmosphere. The rooms also now include fully adjustable desks to accommodate different mobility needs, allowing teens to comfortably do schoolwork or creative activities. Each suite is equipped with the latest state-of-the-art gaming system and adaptive controllers, plus customizable LED lighting that can change colors based on each teen's preferences. Additionally, bathroom updates were made to allow for enhanced comfort and accessibility.

"This renovation speaks to how pediatric care has changed over the past decade and a half," said Dr. Wendy Bernatavicius, medical director at Ryan House and division chief of primary, complex care and adolescent medicine at Phoenix Children's Hospital. "We're seeing more adolescents and young adults with complex needs living vibrant lives. Improved medical care, early intervention and whole-family support have extended the lifespan of many of these teens. That means rethinking the environments where they're cared for. These teen suites are not just rooms. They're an affirmation of identity, belonging, and respect for each young person who calls Ryan House their home away from home."

During their recent holiday break, Ryan House welcomed two familiar guests, brothers Mason and Jaxton, who are living with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, as the first teens to stay in the newly renovated suites. While they've stayed at Ryan House before, this visit marked the first time they experienced the upgraded rooms designed specifically with teens, like them, in mind.

"The gift of these teen suites is the culmination of our vision for this next chapter," said Tracy Leonard Warner, executive director of Ryan House. "This home has been serving families

+ GILBANE INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ GILBANE NAMED ONE OF AMERICA'S BEST EMPLOYERS FOR COMPANY CULTURE 2026

Providence, RI - April 21, 2026 - Gilbane has been named to Forbes' America's Best Employers for Company Culture 2026, recognizing the company's continued commitment to building a culture where people are supported, challenged, and empowered to grow, lead, and perform at a high level.

"A strong culture helps people do their best work and deliver strong results," said Ed Broderick, CEO of Gilbane. "At Gilbane, we create an environment where people are trusted to lead, take ownership, stay accountable and keep growing. Our teams hold a high bar, support each other in meeting it, and bring real care to the work and to one another. That is what drives stronger outcomes for our teams, our clients, and communities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ANNOUNCES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS AS HEAD OF CAPITAL MARKETS

New York, NY (April 20, 2026) - Gilbane Development, a nationally recognized leader in integrated real estate development, today announced the appointment of Andreas Vlahakis as Head of Capital Markets based out of the firm's New York City office. Vlahakis brings extensive experience in real estate capital markets, including a track record spanning all asset classes with a focus on ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.

As Head of Capital Markets, Vlahakis will refine and execute Gilbane Development's funding strategy, reporting directly to James Patchett, President and CEO of Gilbane Development. Vlahakis will help set the firm's capital strategy and make investment decisions, develop and strengthen relationships with funding and equity partners, and lead transaction structuring and negotiations.

"Andreas' appointment reflects our commitment to building the infrastructure needed to support our next phase of growth," said James Patchett, President and CEO at Gilbane Development. "His long-term industry relationships and his deep experience raising and structuring capital will be invaluable as we continue to expand our pipeline and pursue new opportunities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE WELCOMES CHRIS BIRDYSHAW AS BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT PROFESSIONAL IN NEW JERSEY

Newark, NJ - February 18, 2026 - Gilbane Building, a leading global builder, has welcomed Chris Birdyshaw as Business Development Professional in New Jersey, reinforcing the company's continued growth and long-term investment in the state. In this role, Chris will strengthen client and industry partnerships and expand Gilbane's presence across key market sectors.

Chris brings more than 20 years of business development leadership experience in commercial construction and real estate. He has successfully built and maintained strong relationships with owners, developers, architects, engineers, and industry partners across New Jersey and Eastern Pennsylvania. His experience spans life sciences, healthcare, higher education, K-12, multifamily, industrial, and cultural/entertainment sectors.

"Chris's extensive market knowledge and established relationships throughout New Jersey make him a valuable addition to our team," said Ted Holt, Senior Vice President, Mid-Atlantic Division, Gilbane Building. "His strategic approach and commitment to client service align with Gilbane's focus on delivering exceptional outcomes and long-term value."

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.

Source: Company Website

value.Title: MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - THE RECENT SECTOR HEADLINES WITH PEERS

urn:contentItem:6JHK-F5B3-RRV5-4396-00000-00MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - THE RECENT SECTOR HEADLINES WITH PEERS2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:44:21ZLexisNexisSTOCKMCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. - THE RECENT SECTOR HEADLINES WITH PEERS

DASHBOARD: MCCARTHY HOLDINGS

PermID4296686586Websitehttp://www.mccarthy.com/IndustryConstruction & EngineeringAddress1604 Eastport Plaza Drive, Suite 106 (Map it) Collinsville, Illinois 62234

ACTIVITIES:

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc., founded in 1864, is an employee-owned construction firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 7,000 employees, it provides preconstruction and construction services for healthcare, commercial, and renewable energy projects, generating $5.5 billion in 2023 revenue. Notable projects include the Mayo Clinic expansion. McCarthys focus on safety, quality, and technology, including BIM, ensures efficient project delivery. Serving clients like Kaiser Permanente, it competes with Skanska while fostering a collaborative culture and sustainability initiatives, maintaining a strong presence in the U.S. construction market.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS [3 News That Matters]

+ AUDDIA AND THRAMANN MERGE TO ESTABLISH MCCARTHY FINNEY HOLDING

Auddia Inc. has announced a definitive merger agreement with Thramann Holdings to form a new Delaware holding company named McCarthy Finney, Inc. The announcement was made on 17th February 2026, and the details of the merger were disclosed on 18th February 2026. Under this agreement, both Auddia and Thramann will become wholly owned subsidiaries of McCarthy Finney, with Auddia shareholders receiving one common share of McCarthy Finney for each share they hold in Auddia, while Thramann owners will receive a combination of special preferred stock and $3.5 million in unsecured notes.Post-merger, Auddia shareholders are projected to own approximately 20% of McCarthy Finney, while former Thramann stakeholders are expected to hold around 80%, contingent on Auddia maintaining a minimum cash balance of $12 million. The special preferred stock issued to Thramann will carry significant governance rights, including a liquidation preference of at least $20.5 million, alongside flexible conversion and redemption features, giving Thramann substantial influence over the newly formed entity.Both Auddia and Thramann's boards have unanimously approved the merger, with Auddia's independent special committee affirming the deal's fairness, supported by a fairness opinion from Houlihan Capital. Governance continuity is assured, with Auddia's four directors set to join the McCarthy Finney board, while Jeff Thramann and John Mahoney will continue as CEO and CFO, respectively. Additionally, Auddia's executives and directors have committed to support the deal, and Thramann will adhere to a 180-day lock-up on his shares in McCarthy Finney.In terms of valuation, Auddia estimates a base-case worth of $250 million for McCarthy Finney, based on a management-prepared discounted cash flow analysis of the combined entity's pro forma over the next decade. The merger is anticipated to close in the second quarter of 2026, pending Auddia shareholder approval, SEC effectiveness of a Form S-4 filing, and meeting Nasdaq listing requirements. The formation of McCarthy Finney positions the company as a diversified holding entity focused on artificial intelligence applications across distributed AI data centers, healthtech value-based care, and AI-driven travel services.Auddia, based in Boulder, Colorado, is recognized for its innovative audio technology that leverages a proprietary AI platform to enhance consumer engagement with AM/FM radio, podcasts, and other audio content. Its flagship product, the 'faidr' superapp, aims to modernize music consumption and promotion by offering ad-free listening, content and ad-break skipping, and integrated artist discovery.

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF $3.7B CALIFORNIA TOWER

McCarthy Holdings has reached a significant construction milestone with the topping out of the $3.7 billion California Tower on February 6, 2026. This achievement comes a year and a half after breaking ground on the ambitious project in 2024, as reported by UC Davis Health. The California Tower is designed using a progressive design-build delivery method, marking a pivotal moment in the expansion of UC Davis' Sacramento campus.The California Tower is part of an extensive eight-year plan to double the size of the UC Davis campus, which is recognized as the largest health system capital expansion currently underway in the United States. Once completed, the 14-story hospital and five-story pavilion will house operating rooms, an imaging center, and updated facilities dedicated to pharmacy and burn care units. Notably, the tower will also introduce approximately 334 additional inpatient beds to enhance patient care capabilities.In a customary celebration, stakeholders in the project gathered to sign the beam, which was ceremonially placed to mark this important milestone. UC Davis Health anticipates the opening of the California Tower in 2030. This state-of-the-art building is the final component of UC Davis Health's Vision 2030 plan, which aims to increase the campus's total building space from 3.6 million square feet to over 7 million square feet.The California Tower is not McCarthy Holdings' only recent achievement in the healthcare sector. The company completed the Plaza West Tower at Barnes-Jewish Hospital in St. Louis in December. As the Colorado River faces unprecedented challenges, PCL Construction Enterprises, Inc. is stepping up to provide innovative solutions aimed at water resilience. Lake Powell, for instance, is at an alarming 162 feet below full capacity, holding only 26% of its total water volume. The U.S. Bureau of Reclamation warns that if current conditions persist, Lake Mead could reach record lows within two years, prompting a critical reassessment of water management strategies across the basin states.PCL Construction is at the forefront of this reassessment, advocating for investments in water reuse infrastructure as a key strategy for bolstering water security. With projected U.S. investments in water reuse expected to average $47.1 billion over the next decade, PCL is positioned to support municipalities in capturing and reusing water to mitigate the effects of ongoing drought. Although adoption rates vary significantly among the seven Colorado River basin states, PCL's initiatives exemplify how infrastructure investments can enhance resilience in water supply systems.The urgency of the situation is underscored by the imminent expiration of current water management rules in the fall of 2026. As states grapple with the looming deadline to ratify Colorado River water allocation agreements, PCL Construction's projects, such as the Drought Pipeline Program in Phoenix, provide tangible examples of how communities can adapt to a drier future. This program has been crucial in ensuring the reliability of water supply for the rapidly growing Phoenix area, which has seen a 12% population increase over the last decade.Industry leaders, including PCL, recently gathered at the Colorado River Water Users Association Conference to discuss collaborative strategies for securing the river's future. The consensus was clear: a multifaceted approach is necessary to address the complexities of climate change and aging infrastructure. PCL Construction's commitment to advancing water reuse initiatives positions the company as a critical player in shaping sustainable water management practices that can withstand the challenges posed by the Colorado River's ongoing crisis.

+ PCL CONSTRUCTION ADVANCES $192M AI-READY DATA CENTER PROJECT

PCL Construction Services, Inc. and PCL Industrial Services, Inc., operating companies of PCL Construction, have reached structural completion on a $192 million, 22.5-megawatt data center, advancing one of the largest digital infrastructure projects currently underway in the Denver metropolitan area. On 19th March 2026, the companies celebrated this significant milestone that underscores their commitment to delivering state-of-the-art facilities designed for high-capacity digital workloads.Located on a 17-acre site southeast of Denver, the project is being delivered by PCL’s Minneapolis Buildings group in collaboration with the Denver team, combining longstanding client relationships with local market expertise. The data center is specifically designed to support enterprise-scale, compute-intensive workloads, including artificial intelligence and graphics processing unit applications, as demand for high-capacity digital infrastructure continues to grow across the region.PCL’s scope of work includes the phased construction of five steel-frame colocation data halls totaling approximately 277,000 square feet, allowing for efficient onboarding of new tenants while maintaining flexibility for future expansion. The project also features a dedicated administration building with office space for personnel and on-site operations teams. This design aims to accommodate high-density deployments, aligning performance needs with modern sustainability and energy-efficiency requirements.The project includes comprehensive electrical infrastructure to support continuous, high-performance operations, from permanent and temporary power systems to sitewide distribution, security, and communications pathways. Key work includes installing and integrating major power and cooling equipment that enables reliable, high-density data center operations. PCL Industrial Services, Inc. is self-performing the core electrical work while coordinating specialized partners for testing and life-safety systems.PCL Construction’s extensive experience in delivering mission-critical and data center facilities across the United States is critical on complex, high-density projects like this, according to Ryan Schmidt, vice president and district manager of Denver Buildings. The project teams recently marked the topping-out ceremony, celebrating the dedication, craftsmanship, and collaboration that will transition the development into interior build-out and systems installation, with construction scheduled for completion in summer 2027.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE SECURES JOBS GRANT FOR MAJOR HQ EXPANSION

In a significant move to bolster local economic development, the Jefferson County Commission has approved a jobs grant for Brasfield & Gorrie, a prominent construction firm headquartered in Birmingham. On 25th March 2026, the Commission unanimously passed the resolution, which supports the company’s expansion plans that include a new office tower, a parking deck expansion, a training and event center, and a wellness center.Commission President Jimmie Stephens emphasized the importance of Brasfield & Gorrie to the region, noting that the company has been a stalwart presence in Birmingham since its inception. "They’ve chosen to remain here although they are a worldwide company," he remarked, highlighting the firm's commitment to the local economy.The expansion represents a substantial investment of $64,584,500 from Brasfield & Gorrie, with $2,884,500 allocated for land acquisition, $56,100,000 designated for construction, and $5,600,000 earmarked for equipment. This development is expected to create 80 new jobs, each offering an hourly wage of $39.51. The county’s jobs grant, capped at $240,000, is designed to incentivize this growth.In return for its investment, Jefferson County anticipates a return of $8,000 to the general fund and $1,011,773 to the education fund, illustrating the potential benefits of supporting local businesses like Brasfield & Gorrie. Stephens expressed gratitude for the confidence the company has shown in the county, vowing to maintain a strong partnership moving forward.As the economic landscape continues to evolve, the expansion of Brasfield & Gorrie underscores the importance of local support for sustainable growth. The firm’s decision to invest in Birmingham not only strengthens its operational base but also contributes positively to the broader community.

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE UNVEILS $64.5 MILLION EXPANSION PLANS FOR BIRMINGHAM HQ

Brasfield & Gorrie, one of Birmingham’s largest construction firms, is planning a major expansion at its national headquarters, a move company leaders say reflects continued growth and a long-term commitment to the city. The company presented plans to the Birmingham City Council committee on 10th March 2026 for a new office building and parking expansion at its headquarters campus along 7th Avenue South.The proposal outlines a project expected to bring millions of dollars in investment and dozens of new jobs to the city. Brasfield & Gorrie plans to construct a new four-story office building totaling roughly 80,000 square feet. According to company representatives, the first floor will serve as an event and training center designed to host gatherings and professional development opportunities, featuring a large banquet room, an auditorium, and several meeting and training rooms.The remaining three floors are planned for office space that will support Brasfield & Gorrie’s expanding national operations. Company leaders say the additional space will allow them to accommodate new employees while leaving room for future growth. In addition to the new building, Brasfield & Gorrie plans to add a fourth level to its existing parking deck, creating approximately 125 additional parking spaces for employees and visitors.As part of the expansion, Brasfield & Gorrie plans to create 80 new full-time jobs headquartered in Birmingham, with an expected average salary of about $82,000. During the council committee discussion, company leaders emphasized that the new roles will help support the firm’s operations across the country while keeping its headquarters firmly rooted in Birmingham. They stated, 'It will be jobs headquartered here in Birmingham to support our field and our operations. We’re really growing, and we’re committed to staying in Birmingham.'The project represents an estimated $64.5 million investment. Under the proposed agreement, the City of Birmingham would provide financial incentives of up to $80,000 to support the project. The proposal received approval from the council committee and will now move to the full Birmingham City Council for final consideration. If approved, company leaders say construction on the expansion could be completed by late 2027.

+ BRASFIELD & GORRIE RECEIVES $12M TAX INCENTIVE FOR JEFFERSON COUNTY PROJECT

Birmingham-based construction firm Brasfield & Gorrie has secured a substantial economic incentive package from state officials, aimed at bolstering its $65 million project in Jefferson County. On 20th February 2026, the Alabama Department of Commerce approved nearly $12 million in tax credits for the company, which includes $9.69 million in investment tax credits and $2.24 million in jobs tax credits.The incentives are part of a strategic initiative to promote economic development in the region and are expected to play a significant role in the execution of Brasfield & Gorrie's ambitious project. This funding will not only support the immediate development but also contribute to job creation and local investment, which aligns with the state's broader economic goals.Brasfield & Gorrie, a privately held firm recognized for its extensive portfolio in construction and project management, has been a key player in Alabama's construction landscape. The approval of these tax credits underscores the state's commitment to fostering growth within its local businesses, particularly in the construction sector, which has faced challenges amid fluctuating economic conditions.The $64.6 million project will likely enhance Brasfield & Gorrie's operational capabilities and expand its service offerings, further solidifying its market position. As the company moves forward with the development, stakeholders will be keenly observing how these incentives translate into tangible benefits for both Brasfield & Gorrie and the Jefferson County community.In conclusion, the Alabama Department of Commerce's support through this tax incentive package marks a significant milestone for Brasfield & Gorrie, reinforcing its role in driving economic progress in the region. The anticipated outcomes of this project will not only reflect on the company's growth trajectory but also on the overall economic vitality of Birmingham and its surrounding areas.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION UNVEILS FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ENHANCE MANUFACTURING CAPABILITIES

JE Dunn Construction has launched its new off-site manufacturing initiative, Form Off-Site Solutions(TradeMark), designed to specialize in Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. This strategic move comes as the company seeks to capitalize on its century-long legacy in Kansas City and beyond. The introduction of Form Off-Site Solutions is positioned as a response to the growing demand for advanced manufacturing capabilities, especially in a rapidly evolving construction landscape. The launch date of this initiative is 6th April 2026.Led by Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim, Form Off-Site Solutions brings together skilled craftspeople, advanced automation, and innovative strategies to design and produce specialized components for a diverse array of stakeholders, including owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers. The offerings are categorized into four distinct product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, ensuring that various market needs are met with tailored solutions.The end-to-end service model of Form Off-Site Solutions addresses the multifaceted challenges inherent in construction projects. By providing capabilities that range from estimating and design to manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, JE Dunn Construction aims to deliver improved installation efficiency, enhanced project safety and quality, and maximized cost certainty. This comprehensive approach is intended to alleviate labor shortages and accelerate project timelines, critical factors in today's construction environment.Effenheim emphasizes the collaborative nature of Form's operations, stating, "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart." This commitment to collaboration and innovation is echoed by JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford, who notes that the development of Form Off-Site Solutions represents a significant advancement in the company's ongoing quest to drive innovation for its clients.With production facilities located in Kansas City and Nashville, Form Off-Site Solutions is poised to serve clients on a national scale. The company's focus on innovation and strategic thinking is a cornerstone of its operations, aimed at enhancing project safety and quality while also addressing labor shortages in the industry. As JE Dunn Construction continues to push the boundaries of off-site manufacturing, the launch of Form Off-Site Solutions marks a pivotal step in its mission to deliver projects with greater efficiency and certainty.

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION CELEBRATES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE FOR WORKFORCE CENTER

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — Construction has reached a key milestone on the Northland Workforce Development Center, as JE Dunn Construction and project partners recently celebrated the topping out of the new facility. The event, held on 19th March 2026, marked the placement of the final structural steel beam for the project, signaling significant progress on the 145,000-square-foot, two-story building designed to expand access to career and technical education across the Northland region.Prior to the beam’s installation, attendees participated in a traditional beam-signing ceremony, adding their names and messages to a steel beam bearing project and partner logos. This moment underscored the collaboration among the Platte County R-3 School District, partner school districts, project stakeholders, and the broader community in advancing a shared workforce development vision. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, emphasized the importance of this collaboration, stating, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The Northland Workforce Development Center is designed to support programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services. Once complete, the facility will expand the capacity of Northland Career Center and its partners, serving both high school students and adult learners seeking industry-aligned training and credentials. Planned program spaces include flexible labs and classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplace conditions.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening anticipated following the structural milestone. Brian Noller, Executive Director of Business Services and NWDC Campaign Lead at Platte County School District, highlighted the collaborative efforts, stating, 'With support from the State of Missouri, local municipalities, businesses and industries, individual investors, and community foundations, we are making a long-term investment in the success of our students and the future of our region.'JE Dunn is serving as construction manager for the project, working in partnership with the school district and design firm Clark & Enersen. The project team has emphasized safety, schedule adherence, and coordination throughout construction, while maintaining communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities. The Northland Workforce Development Center represents a regional investment in workforce readiness, aligning educational infrastructure with evolving industry needs and supporting long-term economic development in the Kansas City area.

+ CHRIS CHELETTE REJOINS JE DUNN TO BOLSTER HEALTHCARE SECTOR

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025.During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region.In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction.Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively.As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

urn:contentItem:6JGX-CJ33-RTFS-44T9-00000-00ALBERICI CORPORATION - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS2026-05-03T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:52:07ZLexisNexisSTOCKALBERICI CORPORATION - INDUSTRY UPDATES AND PEER TRENDS

DASHBOARD: ALBERICI CORPORATION

PermID5000372176Websitehttp://www.alberici.comIndustryConstructionAddress120 S Central Ave CLAYTON MISSOURI 63105-1705 United States

ACTIVITIES:

Alberici Corporation, founded in 1918, is a construction and engineering firm headquartered in St. Louis, Missouri. With over 2,000 employees, it serves automotive, energy, and healthcare sectors, generating approximately $2 billion annually. Alberici provides design-build, construction management, and general contracting, with notable projects like the Gateway Arch visitor center renovation. Its use of Building Information Modeling (BIM) and sustainable practices enhances efficiency. In 2024, Alberici expanded into renewable energy projects, including solar and wind facilities. The firms culture emphasizes safety, innovation, and employee training, fostering long-term client partnerships. Competing with Turner Construction, Alberici stands out for its quality and environmental stewardship, driving growth in sustainable construction.

+ ALBERICI CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ ALBERICI CORPORATION PROGRESSIVE DESIGN-BUILD IN ACTION: DELIVERING CRITICAL WATER INFRASTRUCTURE AT INDUSTRIAL SPEED

As industrial growth and aging infrastructure place new demands on water systems, project teams are rethinking how critical water infrastructure is delivered. Progressive design-build reduces risk, accelerates delivery, and unlocks innovative solutions on complex water projects.

Source: Company Website

+ ALBERICI CORPORATION WRIGHT CITY UNVEILS NEW WASTEWATER TREATMENT FACILITY

WRIGHT CITY, Mo. - A new wastewater treatment facility is now complete in Wright City, Mo., marking a major step forward in supporting regional growth and sustainability. The project was delivered by the joint venture design-build team of Alberici and Woodard & Curran for the Public Water Supply District No. 2 (PWSD No. 2) in partnership with American Foods Group (AFG).

The new facility expands treatment capacity from 500,000 gallons per day to 3.5 million gallons per day, providing advanced nutrient removal for both municipal and industrial flows. It replaces an older system and supports operations at AFG's new America's Heartland Packaging plant while accommodating future residential and commercial growth.

"The Alberici-Woodard & Curran team worked closely with PWSD No. 2 and AFG to bring this project to life," said John Grib, Vice President at Alberici. "Delivering a complex facility like this ahead of schedule and with zero safety incidents is a true reflection of the collaboration and commitment shared by everyone involved."

The project achieved substantial completion one week ahead of schedule and logged more than 321,000 work hours with zero recordable or lost-time injuries.

PWSD No. 2, AFG, and local leaders recently gathered to celebrate the ribbon cutting at the new facility, located at 1000 Lagoon Drive in Wright City.

Source: Company Website

+ ALBERICI CORPORATION SARONIC EXPANDS ITS LOUISIANA SHIPYARD WITH $300 MILLION INVESTMENT TO ACCELERATE AUTONOMOUS SHIP PRODUCTION

Franklin, Louisiana - Saronic today announced a $300 million investment to expand its Franklin, Louisiana shipyard - a major milestone in the company's continued growth as a leader in autonomous shipbuilding. This investment will significantly increase production capacity for Saronic's fleet of Autonomous Surface Vessels (ASVs), adding 1,500 skilled jobs and strengthening the Gulf Coast's role in the future of American maritime innovation in the process.

Supported by close partnerships with Louisiana Economic Development, St. Mary Parish, and other state and local officials, Saronic is expanding its facility to meet surging demand for its autonomous ships. The company broke ground on the new construction project in November 2025, which will add more than 300,000 square feet of new production capacity. The project is slated for completion by the end of 2026, with expanded operations coming online in early 2027.

"This expansion represents what American industry can achieve when innovation and production are fully aligned," said Dino Mavrookas, Saronic Co-founder and CEO. "Our expanded shipyard will enable us to deliver autonomous ships at unprecedented speed and scale while creating 1,500 high-quality jobs across Louisiana. Together with our next-generation Port Alpha shipyard, we're establishing the modern blueprint for American shipbuilding - an integrated ecosystem that connects autonomy innovation with large-scale production capacity to strengthen and sustain America's maritime leadership for generations to come."

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ KIEWIT CORPORATION [3 Press Releases]

+ NNSA ANNOUNCES APPROVAL TO BEGIN FULL CONSTRUCTION OF SRS SPECIALIZED TRAINING FACILITY FOR PLUTONIUM PIT PRODUCTION VIEW ARTICLE

The U.S. Department of Energy's National Nuclear Security Administration (NNSA) has announced approval of start of construction and established a project baseline, known as Critical Decision (CD)-2/3 approval, for the High-Fidelity Training and Operations Center (HFTOC) at the Savannah River Site (SRS).

The HFTOC will mimic the capabilities in the Savannah River Plutonium Processing Facility (SRPPF) Main Process Building and allow operators to develop pit production competencies on "like-for-like" sets of equipment with surrogate materials. The capabilities at HFTOC are essential to develop SRS' pit production workforce and will expedite the production of war reserve plutonium pits once SRPPF comes online.

Source: Company Website

+ PROJET TRAMCITE SELECTS CONSTRUCTION KIEWIT CIE FOR UNDERGROUND UTILITY RELOCATION WORK

Projet TramCite has reached a new milestone with the selection of Kiewit, supported by CIMA+, to carry out underground utility relocation work on Rene-Levesque Boulevard and 1st Avenue in Quebec City. This work represents a key preparatory phase for the implementation of the future tramway.

Kiewit was selected following a rigorous and structured procurement process conducted in accordance with the highest industry standards. The company will rely on the technical expertise and execution capacity of its teams to deliver this complex scope of work. Active in Quebec since 1957, Kiewit has an established track record in the delivery of major infrastructure projects, including the reconstruction of the Turcot Interchange in Montreal, the extension of Highway 25, and the construction of hydroelectric facilities.

The underground utility relocation work will prepare two strategic downtown corridors for the future tramway. Particular attention will be paid to managing impacts and implementing mitigation measures for residents, businesses, and users of the area.

Source: Company Website

+ OGLETHORPE POWER SELECTS KIEWIT SUBSIDIARY FOR NEW MONROE COUNTY COMBINED-CYCLE PLANT

Tucker, GA (January 13, 2026) - Today, Oglethorpe Power announced it has reached another major milestone in the development of its new combined-cycle natural gas power plant in Monroe County, officially selecting Kiewit Corporation through its subsidiary, The Industrial Company (TIC), as the Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) partner for the project. Owned by and serving 38 Electric Membership Cooperatives (EMCs) across Georgia, Oglethorpe Power is developing the new facility to support long-term, system-wide load growth across its cooperative membership, including homes, businesses and existing industries-not to serve any single customer or standalone project.

"We are proud to partner with Kiewit through its subsidiary TIC for this critical energy project that will support our mission to deliver safe, reliable and affordable energy to our Members and the millions of Georgians they serve," said Annalisa Bloodworth, Oglethorpe Power President & CEO. "Its proven success in delivering complex power projects safely, on schedule and within budget, combined with a commitment to safety and community, makes the company an ideal partner for this facility and aligns perfectly with our cooperative principles and dedication to Monroe County."

The TIC team is expected to mobilize in Spring 2026, with commercial operation of the new facility anticipated in 2029. Once online, the 1,425-megawatt plant will provide reliable, efficient electricity to homes and businesses across Georgia served by Oglethorpe Power's Member cooperatives.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 Press Releases]

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION HASLAM SPORTS GROUP, AECOM HUNT AND TURNER JV BREAK GROUND ON HUNTINGTON BANK FIELD

Construction of New State-of-the-Art Facility to Serve as Ohio's First Enclosed Stadium Revolutionary Design by HKS to Bring Football Fans Closer to the Field Than EverThe Haslam Sports Group (HSG) alongside a joint venture of AECOM Hunt and Turner Construction Company and HKS today announced the groundbreaking of the new Huntington Bank Field, the future home of the Cleveland Browns starting in the 2029 season. The groundbreaking ceremony held at the new site in Brook Park today marks the official start of construction on Northeast Ohio's largest economic development project to date, the new Huntington Bank Field enclosed stadium and surrounding district.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION WHERE SMALL CHANGES CREATE REAL IMPACT

Turner's support to the Paraplegic Foundation reflects a focus on improving everyday spaces at Sanjeevandeep Special School. The initiative has contributed to creating a more accessible, comfortable environment for children and caregivers.Our recent association with the Paraplegic Foundation came from a shared belief that everyone deserves the opportunity to live with dignity, comfort, and as much independence as possible. Through their work, the Foundation supports initiatives like Sanjeevandeep Special School, a space dedicated to children with special needs, where care, learning, and rehabilitation come together in a very real, hands-on way.

Source: Company Website

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION: CONNECTING YOUNG TALENT WITH INDUSTRY LEADERS

As part of our Young Professionals Employee Resource Group initiatives, we hosted the first session of our Coffee Talks with Industry Leaders series, with the participation of Arup Turkiye Country Manager Tulay Hatirnaz, Associate Director Duygu Tokalakoglu, and Associate Aydan Basyazicioglu. Held on April 10 at the Arup Istanbul office, the session brought together our young professionals from across Turkiye. Our esteemed speakers shared their industry experience, career journeys, and leadership perspectives, while also engaging with participants through an interactive Q&A.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. [4 Press Releases]

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES TOPS OUT GILEAD SCIENCES' NEW TECHNICAL DEVELOPMENT CENTER IN FOSTER CITY

McCarthy Building Companies, Inc. (McCarthy), recently celebrated the topping out of the New Technical Development Center (NTDC) at Gilead Sciences' headquarters in Foster City, marking a major construction milestone with the completion of the building's structural frame. The work is in collaboration with design partners DGA Architects, Salas O'Brien Structural Engineers and civil designer BKF Engineer.

Project partners, field teams and leaders gathered on site to celebrate the center's topping out, marking placement of the final beam and the structure reaching full height. The centuries-old tradition honors the collective effort behind the milestone and signals that structural work is nearing completion as the project moves into its next phase.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: EXPANDING HOSPITAL PARKING STRUCTURES FOR BETTER CARE

Driving your child to the hospital is already a harrowing experience. Finding parking once you arrive should not be your biggest concern. When parking structures prioritize flexibility, enhance connectivity, and respond to evolving patient and staff needs, they make hospital visits a little less daunting.

In early 2025, as McCarthy Building Companies completed the Southwest Tower at Children's Hospital of Orange County (CHOC), in Orange, California, the construction firm also expanded the facility's parking structure. This project involved expanding the structure from 522,000 to 912,000 square feet by adding three vertical levels as well as another horizontal bay to connect the new outpatient tower and the existing main campus. However, the expansion was about more than connecting buildings. It increased the efficiency and sustainability of the overall structure by accommodating a growing number of patients and staff within this care-centric environment.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY HOLDINGS: BRIGHT FUTURE: A'JEYA ANTHONY'S PATH FROM NSBE TO MCCARTHY

Our goal to be the best builder in America requires an inclusive talent acquisition strategy that enables us to find and recruit the best talent. By expanding our reach through organizations like the National Society of Black Engineers (NSBE), we can connect with emerging leaders and create more pathways into our industry.

A'Jeya Anthony is a three-time project intern at McCarthy Building Companies. Her journey is just one example of how meeting top candidates where they are help us to connect with exceptional people who strengthen our workforce and business.

Source: Company Website

+ MCCARTHY BUILDING COMPANIES, INC. RYAN HOUSE DEBUTS TEEN SUITES FOR KIDS LIVING LONGER WITH LIFE-LIMITING CONDITIONS

Ryan House, a nonprofit that provides respite and end-of-life care to children with life-limiting conditions, recently capped off its 15th anniversary year by unveiling two newly renovated teen suites. The renovations allow Ryan House to better serve children who are now surviving well into adolescence and young adulthood and were made possible through generous support from McCarthy Building Companies, which selected Ryan House as a recipient of the company's Heart Hats(TradeMark) grants, and ARC Document Solutions.

Thanks to advances in diagnostics, disease management, and comprehensive care, many children with life-limiting conditions are now living longer than once expected. Ryan House is evolving alongside the children and families it serves, making meaningful changes to better support teens through environments designed specifically for this stage of life.

The renovation was spearheaded by McCarthy's Specialized Solutions Group, which focuses on complex renovations and technical builds within healthcare and other specialized environments. The donation involved the renovation of two private suites specifically designed for teenagers and features superhero-themed decor provided by ARC that fully immerses them in a comic book-inspired atmosphere. The rooms also now include fully adjustable desks to accommodate different mobility needs, allowing teens to comfortably do schoolwork or creative activities. Each suite is equipped with the latest state-of-the-art gaming system and adaptive controllers, plus customizable LED lighting that can change colors based on each teen's preferences. Additionally, bathroom updates were made to allow for enhanced comfort and accessibility.

"This renovation speaks to how pediatric care has changed over the past decade and a half," said Dr. Wendy Bernatavicius, medical director at Ryan House and division chief of primary, complex care and adolescent medicine at Phoenix Children's Hospital. "We're seeing more adolescents and young adults with complex needs living vibrant lives. Improved medical care, early intervention and whole-family support have extended the lifespan of many of these teens. That means rethinking the environments where they're cared for. These teen suites are not just rooms. They're an affirmation of identity, belonging, and respect for each young person who calls Ryan House their home away from home."

During their recent holiday break, Ryan House welcomed two familiar guests, brothers Mason and Jaxton, who are living with Duchenne muscular dystrophy, as the first teens to stay in the newly renovated suites. While they've stayed at Ryan House before, this visit marked the first time they experienced the upgraded rooms designed specifically with teens, like them, in mind.

"The gift of these teen suites is the culmination of our vision for this next chapter," said Tracy Leonard Warner, executive director of Ryan House. "This home has been serving families

+ GILBANE INC. [3 Press Releases]

+ GILBANE NAMED ONE OF AMERICA'S BEST EMPLOYERS FOR COMPANY CULTURE 2026

Providence, RI - April 21, 2026 - Gilbane has been named to Forbes' America's Best Employers for Company Culture 2026, recognizing the company's continued commitment to building a culture where people are supported, challenged, and empowered to grow, lead, and perform at a high level.

"A strong culture helps people do their best work and deliver strong results," said Ed Broderick, CEO of Gilbane. "At Gilbane, we create an environment where people are trusted to lead, take ownership, stay accountable and keep growing. Our teams hold a high bar, support each other in meeting it, and bring real care to the work and to one another. That is what drives stronger outcomes for our teams, our clients, and communities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE DEVELOPMENT ANNOUNCES ANDREAS VLAHAKIS AS HEAD OF CAPITAL MARKETS

New York, NY (April 20, 2026) - Gilbane Development, a nationally recognized leader in integrated real estate development, today announced the appointment of Andreas Vlahakis as Head of Capital Markets based out of the firm's New York City office. Vlahakis brings extensive experience in real estate capital markets, including a track record spanning all asset classes with a focus on ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.

As Head of Capital Markets, Vlahakis will refine and execute Gilbane Development's funding strategy, reporting directly to James Patchett, President and CEO of Gilbane Development. Vlahakis will help set the firm's capital strategy and make investment decisions, develop and strengthen relationships with funding and equity partners, and lead transaction structuring and negotiations.

"Andreas' appointment reflects our commitment to building the infrastructure needed to support our next phase of growth," said James Patchett, President and CEO at Gilbane Development. "His long-term industry relationships and his deep experience raising and structuring capital will be invaluable as we continue to expand our pipeline and pursue new opportunities."

Source: Company Website

+ GILBANE WELCOMES CHRIS BIRDYSHAW AS BUSINESS DEVELOPMENT PROFESSIONAL IN NEW JERSEY

Newark, NJ - February 18, 2026 - Gilbane Building, a leading global builder, has welcomed Chris Birdyshaw as Business Development Professional in New Jersey, reinforcing the company's continued growth and long-term investment in the state. In this role, Chris will strengthen client and industry partnerships and expand Gilbane's presence across key market sectors.

Chris brings more than 20 years of business development leadership experience in commercial construction and real estate. He has successfully built and maintained strong relationships with owners, developers, architects, engineers, and industry partners across New Jersey and Eastern Pennsylvania. His experience spans life sciences, healthcare, higher education, K-12, multifamily, industrial, and cultural/entertainment sectors.

"Chris's extensive market knowledge and established relationships throughout New Jersey make him a valuable addition to our team," said Ted Holt, Senior Vice President, Mid-Atlantic Division, Gilbane Building. "His strategic approach and commitment to client service align with Gilbane's focus on delivering exceptional outcomes and long-term value."

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [4 Press Releases]

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC GREEN FROM THE INSIDE OUT: BERKELEY LAB SETS NEW STANDARDS

Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory's Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center sets new sustainability standards. Berkeley Lab Biological and Environmental Program Integration Center (BioEPIC) unites scientists from LBNL's biosciences and earth and environmental sciences areas in a four-story, 71,000-square-foot facility that sets a new standard for sustainability. Completed in March, the facility offers a suite of next-generation tools designed to study how microbial communities interact with plants and soil to influence the environment.

Inside, 200 researchers perform carefully controlled experiments on microbe-soil-plant interactions with instruments and computing infrastructure that connect BioEPIC to its field research sites. These unique climate research environments, along with three additional levels of labs and collaborative spaces, are housed in an all-electric facility designed to maximize energy efficiency and resiliency.

By supporting an array of unique research needs in this highly sustainable setting, the BioEPIC

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC ENHANCING RICHMOND'S WATER RESILIENCE

Clark Water is currently leading improvements on the Shockoe Diversion Structure in Richmond - a key part of the city's combined sewer overflow (CSO) system, which was originally built in the early 1970s. The project will alleviate sewage and stormwater overflow during high-water events. To achieve this, crews have installed mechanical trash rake screens to prevent debris from passing through, two crest gates to minimize upstream flooding, and are making upgrades to supporting infrastructure.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC BUILDERS AT HEART: WINSTON VAN KEULEN

This article is part of our series Builders at Heart, where we highlight the passions and backgrounds of the Clark team - the things that shape us - that allow us to tackle challenges head-on, solve complex problems, and build what matters.

Meet Winston van Keulen, a superintendent with Clark Construction, currently working in San Diego. Discover how he is progressing in his role and the keys to success he shares with new team members.

Source: Company Website

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC FROM CONCEPT TO REALITY: HARNESSING 3D PRINTING AT CLARK'S MID-ATLANTIC YARD

Clark Construction's Mid-Atlantic Yard in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, is a powerhouse of specialized services, from fleet oversight to custom fabrication. This diverse facility is also part of a transformative initiative: the integration of 3D printing for prototyping innovative custom solutions to solve complex challenges.

The method came to life when faced with a challenge: designing a custom cleaning piece for a drill rig auger. While the traditional route would involve costly and time-consuming steel fabrication for a prototype, Charles Harman III, a craft and machine shop supervisor, and his team explored a new solution for this type of fabrication and successfully used a 3D printer to create a scaled model. The successful prototype demonstrated the efficacy of the system, and once the 3D printed model was approved, the team began assembling the steel-fabricated version.
